problem_summary: 2022 MCM Problem C (data): given five years of daily Bitcoin (BCHAIN-MKPRU) and gold (LBMA-GOLD) price series, design a daily trading strategy starting from $1000 that maximizes terminal wealth on 2021-09-10, accounting for transaction costs (2% BTC, 1% gold), prove the strategy is optimal/best, and analyze sensitivity of strategy and final wealth to transaction costs. Team 2224507's answer reframes the problem as a technical-analysis quant strategy: optimize the parameters of seven classical stock-market indicator factors, combine the best factors into a 3-of-5 voting system, size positions with a Markowitz mean-variance model, then layer LSTM price forecasting plus a forced 20% stop-loss on top, reaching a final asset of $190,635.58 (185.49% annualized).
models_used: Technical-indicator decision factors as buy/sell signal generators: KDJ, RSI, WR (overbought/oversold), MACD, TRIX, DMA (trend), BOLL/Bollinger Bands (pressure-support) — 7 classical factors computed from closing price only

Per-factor parameter-optimization model: a discrete mathematical program maximizing terminal assets M_1826 over factor parameters (e.g. RSI period n, buy threshold I, sell threshold O; MACD n1,n2; BOLL n,k), with state-transition constraints linking cash C, holdings B, total M, binary buy/sell indicators p,q (p+q<=1), transaction-cost factor alpha — solved in MATLAB on the FIRST YEAR of data only (in-sample training)
Voting system / ensemble signal: 3-of-5 factor agreement triggers a trade (BTC voters = DMA,RSI,MACD,TRIX,WR; gold voters = DMA,RSI,MACD,TRIX,BOLL)

Markowitz mean-variance portfolio model: minimize portfolio variance (using rolling std of daily returns and BTC-gold covariance) to set position weights H_btc, H_gd when holding both assets, subject to 0<=H_btc+H_gd<=1, H>=0

LSTM neural network for one-step-ahead price forecasting (input/forget/output gates, cell state) — predicted price w'_{t+1} substituted into factor formulas (e.g. DMA*, WR*) so day t+1 signal is available on day t
Forced stop-loss risk-control rule: liquidate when an asset falls >20% from entry

God's-perspective upper-bound LP: a perfect-foresight maximization model (objective Max M_n with full BTC+gold transition constraints) solved in LINGO to get the theoretical maximum $14,362,722,337.67 as a benchmark
key_techniques: In-sample/out-of-sample split: parameters fitted on year 1 (2016-09 to 2017-09), strategy evaluated over all 5 years — a genuine train/test discipline rare in MCM C papers
Recasting an open-ended 'design a strategy' prompt into a parameter-optimization problem with an explicit objective function and state-transition constraints, making it tractable and defensible
Binary indicator variables (p,q) with p+q<=1 and logic that a bought day has C=0 (no double-buy) — clean encoding of trading constraints in the MP
Ensemble voting to denoise individual factor signals, reducing the 13 trades over 5 years (low churn => low transaction-cost drag)

Layered model improvement: baseline voting strategy ($124,716.87) -> add LSTM forecast + stop-loss -> improved strategy ($190,635.58), with explicit before/after delta (+51.64% terminal, +14% they cite)

Three-pronged optimality argument for Task 2: (a) scientific legitimacy of factors from finance literature, (b) parameter-optimization gains >30% over textbook stock parameters, (c) benchmarking vs God's-perspective LP (model achieves 0.0013% of theoretical max) and vs real firms (J.P. Morgan, CICC annual returns)

Transaction-cost sensitivity sweep: vary per-trade cost 0-6% for both single factors and the portfolio, tracking BOTH terminal assets and trade count, showing the portfolio degrades more gracefully than single factors
Multi-tool workflow: MATLAB for factor optimization, LSTM for forecasting, LINGO for the perfect-foresight LP
why_it_won: "From a judge's view, the paper wins on three things MCM rewards. (1) It answers all three sub-questions concretely with numbers: a real $190,635.58 terminal value, a sensitivity sweep, and a genuine optimality argument. (2) The optimality section is unusually strong — instead of just asserting 'our strategy is good', it bounds performance against a perfect-foresight LINGO upper bound ($14.36B) AND against named real-world firms (J.P. Morgan, CICC), giving the verdict credibility. (3) Methodological discipline: training factor parameters on only the first year and testing on the remaining four shows the team understands look-ahead bias and out-of-sample validation, which signals modeling maturity. The layered narrative (single factor -> optimized parameters -> voting ensemble -> LSTM+stop-loss) gives a clear improvement story with quantified deltas at each step, and the low trade count (13 in 5 years) makes the strategy robust to the transaction-cost question that sinks naive high-frequency answers."
section_highlights: "Summary clearly states the headline numbers ($1,000 -> $190,635.58, 185.49% annualized) and the +14% improvement from the LSTM/risk-control layer. Section 1.3 'Our work' uses a full mermaid-style flowchart tying data preprocessing -> factor selection -> optimization -> voting -> forecasting. Section 4.3 is the technical core: it writes out the full MP (eqs 8-15) with line-by-line justification of every constraint (eqs 9-14 each explained in prose). Section 4.4 Table 2 compactly reports optimal parameters + 5-yr assets + annualized yield for all 7 factors x 2 assets — excellent at-a-glance comparison that motivates which factors made the voting cut. Section 5 (Optimality) is the standout structural choice: three independent angles plus the God's-perspective LINGO benchmark and the investment-firm bar chart. Section 6 separates transaction-cost effects on single factors vs the portfolio, plotting assets and trade count together. Closes with a one-page client-facing MEMO restating the strategy in plain trader-facing language."
pitfalls_or_limitations: LSTM forecast is almost certainly overfit / look-ahead-leaky: the predicted vs real price plots (Fig 11) overlap near-perfectly, which for daily crypto prices signals the model is effectively predicting w'_{t+1} ~ w_t (lagged copy), not adding real predictive value — yet they substitute it into factors as if it were a true forecast

Numerical inconsistency in the summary: text says original program ended at $125,716.87 in one place and $124,716.87 elsewhere (Table 4), and claims '14% higher' while Table 4 / Fig 13 imply +51.64% — sloppy number hygiene that a sharp judge will notice
Several figures are corrupted/garbled in the rendering (Fig 2, 5, 6 scatterplot tables contain nonsense monotone columns; Fig 16 axis labels mislabeled), suggesting weak figure quality control

Assumptions are convenient and under-justified: 'no minimum trading unit', 'no systemic risk', and all-in/all-out (buy everything or sell everything) — the all-or-nothing trading rule contradicts the mean-variance position-sizing, which is only partially reconciled

Parameter optimization is done per-factor in isolation on a single 1-year window with no cross-validation or regime-robustness check; the 'optimal' parameters (e.g. RSI n=16,O=75,I=32) are at risk of being curve-fit to 2016-17
The 'God's perspective' benchmark uses perfect foresight with the SAME all-in mechanics, so the 0.0013% ratio is more rhetorical than rigorous
Voting threshold (3-of-5) and the 20% stop-loss level appear chosen by hand, not optimized or justified by sensitivity analysis
reusable_patterns: Recast 'design the best X' into a constrained optimization: write an explicit objective (terminal wealth) plus state-transition constraints with binary action variables — turns a vague design prompt into a solvable, gradeable model
Train-on-window-1, test-on-rest split for any time-series strategy problem to demonstrate awareness of look-ahead bias — cheap to do, strongly rewarded
Ensemble/voting to combine multiple weak signals and reduce action frequency, which directly improves the transaction-cost / robustness sub-question

Three-layer optimality argument template: (a) cite literature for component legitimacy, (b) quantify improvement over a naive baseline, (c) bound against a perfect-foresight upper limit AND a real-world peer benchmark
Build a perfect-information LP (LINGO/solver) purely as a theoretical ceiling to contextualize how good your heuristic strategy is — a clean, reusable optimality device
Sensitivity sweep that tracks two coupled outputs (terminal value AND trade count) vs the cost parameter, and contrasts the ensemble against its components to argue stability
Summary table reporting parameters + outcome + normalized metric (annualized yield) across all model variants in one grid for at-a-glance comparison
Close with a client-facing MEMO that re-states the technical strategy in stakeholder language — matches the ICM/MCM C deliverable expectation
